notes.pro_life: A+ pro-life rating, worked to pass 20-week abortion ban in Congress, defense of life at all stages
notes.former_congressman: Represented ND at-large in US House 2019-2024, resigned Dec 14, 2024 to become governor
notes.nd_gop_chair: Former North Dakota Republican Party chair (2015-2018)
notes.energy: Strong advocate for North Dakota energy sector
notes.commander_in_chief: Serves as commander in chief of ND National Guard as governor

profile.religion: Christian (faith-focused campaign: 'Faith, Family and Conservative Values have guided Kelly throughout his life')
profile.education: University of North Dakota (BA), University of North Dakota School of Law (JD 2003)
profile.background: Attorney; North Dakota state senator (2012-2018); chair of North Dakota Republican Party; U.S. Representative (2019-2024); became 34th governor in 2024.

notes.indian_affairs: Serves on Senate Indian Affairs Committee
notes.fiscal: Generally conservative but pragmatic fiscal record

profile.background: Former Governor of North Dakota (2000-2010) and president of First Western Bank. Longest-serving governor in North Dakota history. In Senate since 2011.
profile.office: 338 Russell Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 220 E. Rosser Ave., Room 312, U.S. Federal Building
102 N. Fourth St., Room 108, Federal Building

aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

notes.second_amendment: Supports national Right-to-Carry reciprocity, co-introduced legislation to block state gun registries with Hyde-Smith
notes.healthcare: Opposes ACA, voted to repeal without replacement 5 times
notes.evangelicals: Meets with evangelical leaders, discusses immigration through 'biblical lens'
notes.energy: Advocates for North Dakota energy sector
notes.fiscal: 96% Heritage Action score in current session - sharply conservative

profile.religion: Evangelical Christian (met with National Association of Evangelicals, approaches policy through 'biblical lens')
profile.education: Concordia College Moorhead, MN (BA), University of Mary Bismarck, ND (MA Management, Honorary Doctor of Leadership 2013)
profile.background: Former North Dakota Public Service Commissioner and state tourism director. Served as state economic development director. U.S. House member from 2013-2019 before winning Senate seat.
profile.office: 313 Hart Senate Office Building

notes.historic: First woman elected to US House from North Dakota
notes.energy: Former president of National Association of Regulatory Utility Commissioners (NARUC), served on ND Public Service Commission 2012-2025
notes.pro_life: Cited abortion as key issue she would focus on as representative
notes.agriculture: Agriculture as priority for ND at-large district
notes.federal_overreach: Leading resolution to overturn Biden-era resource management plan for North Dakota

profile.religion: Catholic (Cathedral of the Holy Spirit congregation, Bismarck - 30-year member)
profile.office: 1607 Longworth House Office Building
